immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped systolic blood-pressure measurement event representing event-level ICU systolic blood pressure rather than a baseline, summary, or stay-level aggregate. The retained asset may expose invasive, noninvasive, and combined representations, but each row still reflects measured systolic blood pressure.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_sbp must preserve this event-level systolic blood-pressure meaning.

canonical_representation.range_role_note: This is the current cleaned retained-output range for the governed MIMIC build, not a claim that every raw source row in every database is already valid.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.note: A later review may lock a blood-pressure semantic anchor after the cross-database event boundary is frozen more formally.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_sbp from event-level systolic blood pressure to baseline systolic blood pressure
changing std_sbp from systolic blood pressure to pulse pressure or another derived hemodynamic construct
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same event-level systolic blood-pressure meaning
locking a reviewed external semantic anchor without changing the variable meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: source code systems and source codes
database-specific timing anchors and local structural keys
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing baseline or stay-level averaged systolic blood pressure as std_sbp
publishing mean arterial pressure or pulse pressure as std_sbp

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_sbp MVP execution should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_sbp outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
